original_hypothesis.title: Unrestricted addDeposit Directs Third-Party Funds to Payer on Channel Finalization
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: addDeposit() has no restriction on msg.sender — anyone can deposit tokens into any channel. Deposited tokens increase channel.deposit, and on finalize() or close(), the remaining balance (deposit - settled) is refunded exclusively to channel.payer. If any address other than the payer deposits tokens (e.g., payee trying to extend channel capacity, or a third party instructed to fund a channel), those funds become irrecoverably directed to the payer on channel closure. The lack of caller restriction combined with addDeposit being allowed during the grace period amplifies this risk.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Missing msg.sender == channel.payer check is a code defect that creates an irreversible fund misdirection path. While exploitation requires the non-payer to voluntarily deposit, the function's permissive interface provides no warning that deposited funds will be claimed by the payer.

proof_of_signal.pattern: addDeposit has no require(msg.sender == channel.payer) and no require(channel.gracePeriodEnd == 0). Any address can deposit at any time, funds go to payer on finalize.

preconditions: A non-payer address calls addDeposit on an existing channel
Channel eventually reaches finalization (via finalize or close)
Remaining funds after settlement are refunded to payer

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: channel.deposit += amount (anyone's deposit); payerRefund = deposit - settled (100% to payer)
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Payee or third party deposits tokens thinking they fund the channel. On finalize, payer gets those tokens as refund.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Payer profits by the amount deposited by non-payer addresses

economic_questions: Is there a legitimate use case for non-payer deposits?
Could a malicious payer social-engineer their payee into depositing?
Should addDeposit be restricted to payer or blocked during grace period?
validation_requirements: Test: non-payer deposits, channel finalizes, verify payer receives non-payer's funds
Test: addDeposit during grace period, verify no restriction

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Equal to amount deposited by non-payer
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Requires social engineering then single finalize tx

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During grace period when payee might panic-deposit

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Non-payer addresses who deposit into channels

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check if any SDK or UI facilitates non-payer deposits
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace addDeposit → finalize fund flow for non-payer

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
